title: Sloanea parviflora
taxonomy: Sloanea parviflora is a species of flowering plant in the family Ebenaceae. It is native to tropical Asia and is also known as the 'sloanea parviflora'. The plant is classified as a species within the genus Sloanea and is further categorized into the kingdom Plantae. Sloanea parviflora is also known to be a part of the class Magnoliopsida and the order Oxalidales. Within its classification, Sloanea parviflora is closely related to other species within the Ebenaceae family.
uses: Sloanea parviflora is used in traditional medicine, particularly in the Philippines and other parts of Southeast Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, with reported health benefits including anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities, which may help to alleviate conditions such as fever and infections.
